items.title: Son mocked over military service and a drone shot down – will South Korea get chaotic World Cup back on track?
items.summary: Hong Myung-bo’s squad has had to deal with issues off the pitch but a point against South Africa is enough to progress The tacos that Son Heung-min ate were reportedly of the Al Pastor and Arrachera variety. items.title: No extra revenue for Fifa from hydration breaks - Infantino
items.summary: Hydration breaks at the World Cup are "purely a sporting matter" and bring "no additional revenue for Fifa", says president Gianni Infantino.

items.title: Ancelotti's quest to end Brazil's 24-year wait for World Cup glory
items.summary: BBC Scotland's Andy Burke looks at Carlo Ancelotti and his mission at Brazil before their final Group C game against Scotland.

items.title: Quiz: How well do you know birthday boy Messi at World Cups?
items.summary: Argentina great Lionel Messi turns 39 today and has been lighting up the World Cup this summer, but how well do you know his journey so far?

items.title: Croatia earn narrow win to knock out Panama
items.summary: Croatia remain in contention for a last-32 spot at the World Cup after earning their first points in Group L with a win over Panama, who are knocked out of the World Cup following the loss.

items.title: Famous DR Congo fan makes first World Cup 2026 appearance for Colombia clash after being forced to quarantine
items.summary: DR CONGO's most iconic fan made his World Cup debut after being forced to miss the African nation's opener due to an Ebola quarantine. items.title: Panama 0 Croatia 1: Budimir goal helps Modric & Co keep pressure on England ahead of final group games
items.summary: CROATIA narrowly saw off Panama to put the pressure on England going into the final fixtures of the Group Stage. items.title: Madrid sign Sweden's Schröder in big-money deal
items.summary: Sweden forward Felicia Schröder has signed with Real Madrid after a major transfer from B.K. Häcken, the two clubs announced on Tuesday.

items.title: Ronaldo snaps goal drought, 1st to score at 6 WCs
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo made history Tuesday by becoming the first player to score in six different World Cups, finding the net in Portugal's 5-0 win over Uzbekistan.

items.title: Kane's late miss sees England frustrated by Ghana
items.summary: Harry Kane missed a golden late chance as England's World Cup campaign was brought back to Earth with a goalless draw against Ghana at Gillette Stadium on Tuesday.

items.title: USMNT's Adams aims to play amid suspension risk
items.summary: Tyler Adams understands a yellow card in Thursday's match against Türkiye would see him suspended for the knockout round match the U.S. has already clinched, he still isn't convinced that sitting out is the right decision.

items.title: Ronaldo underlines legacy, importance to Portugal ...
items.summary: "Why does Cristiano Ronaldo still start?" was a valid question after his dud vs. Congo DR, but he answered it emphatically in Portugal's win on Tuesday.

items.title: Worries exposed for England as Kane looks lost and...
items.summary: England's stats vs. Ghana were dominant -- more possession, more shots, more passes -- but the goals never came, raising concerns for this World Cup.

items.title: Ghana: 'VAR went for coffee' to aid 'lucky' Englan...
items.summary: Ghana boss Carlos Queiroz joked that the video assistant referee must have gone for a coffee after saying England escaped a penalty and red card in Tuesday's goalless World Cup stalemate.

items.title: Sources: Atleti 'furious' with Barça over Álvarez
items.summary: Atlético Madrid are upset with Julián Álvarez's revelation that he wants to leave the club this summer and with Barcelona for pushing the Argentina forward to make a public transfer request, Atleti CEO Miguel Gil Marin said on Tuesday.

items.title: France coach to miss game after mother's death
items.summary: France coach Didier Deschamps will not be on the touchline for Les Bleus' final World Cup group game against Norway on Friday after the death of his mother.

items.title: Mexico players want Ochoa for historic WC start
items.summary: With their spot already guaranteed into the knockout rounds, Mexico players were in full support of a possible historic start for goalkeeping icon Guillermo Ochoa against Czechia in their World Cup group stage finale on Wednesday.

items.title: 🤔 Permutations: How teams qualify for knockouts
items.summary: Who can secure qualification before the group stage ends, and whose World Cup dreams could be shattered? Here's what you need to know.

items.title: USMNT's World Cup success brings dilemma: Rotate t...
items.summary: The USMNT has clinched a spot in the World Cup knockouts, so should the team rotate in its final Group D match or stay consistent?
